title: My QRPer Feature: My KX2 Kit
context: Read the article K4SWL wrote on my KX2 kit
body: I wrote this up for Thomas a while ago now, but it's a set of 3D printed inserts I made for the Seahorse waterproof case to hold my KX2 securely. Still use it in a rather similar form to this day, although I've moved past trying to get the AX2 to work. Compromised station power or compromised antennas, I haven't had success with both.

KO6BYB was my original ham callsign, I got a vanity call N6CBL earlier this year after getting my general license. N6CBL is supposed to be CABLE, which is a nickname I have had at work. Cameron Gable -> Cable -> Cable, it's a good nickname for someone who does AVI systems and all the harnesses, cables, and wires involved.
